draft_reasoning: This account represents raw material consumption of steel in manufacturing, which is clearly direct materials used in the production process. Raw materials are explicitly included in the Purchases of inventory category under ASC 220-40-50-6(b). The functional area confirms this is manufacturing/COGS, and the similar pre-mapped account for raw material purchases provides additional support for this classification.

This account represents raw material consumption of aluminum, which is a direct material cost used in manufacturing. Raw materials are explicitly included in the Purchases of inventory category under ASC 220-40-50-6(b). The functional area is Manufacturing/COGS and the account has a similar pre-mapped account (Raw Material Purchases) classified the same way, providing strong supporting evidence for this classification.

This account represents raw material consumption of copper, which is a direct material cost for manufacturing operations. Raw materials consumed in production clearly fall under the 'Purchases of inventory' category as defined in ASC 220-40-50-6(b), which specifically includes raw materials and direct materials. The functional area designation as Manufacturing/COGS and the similarity to the pre-mapped raw material purchases account further support this classification with high confidence.

This account represents raw material consumption of polymers used in manufacturing, which directly aligns with the 'Purchases of inventory' category under ASC 220-40-50-6(b). Raw materials consumed in production are specifically included in this category as they represent the cost of materials purchased for manufacturing goods. The functional area classification as Manufacturing/COGS and the similar pre-mapped account for raw material purchases further support this categorization with high confidence.

This account represents raw material consumption of chemicals in manufacturing, which directly aligns with the 'Purchases of inventory' category that includes raw materials and direct materials per ASC 220-40-50-6(b). The functional area is Manufacturing/COGS and the account tracks consumption of materials used in production. The similar pre-mapped account for 'Raw Material Purchases' provides additional support for this classification.

This account represents purchased electronic components used in manufacturing, which clearly falls under purchases of inventory as defined by ASC 220-40-50-6(b). These are direct materials purchased for use in production processes. The functional area designation of Manufacturing/COGS and the substantial balance of over $4M confirms this is a core inventory purchase account that should be classified as Cost of Goods Sold.

This account represents purchased mechanical components that are clearly raw materials or direct materials used in manufacturing, which fall directly under the 'Purchases of inventory' category per ASC 220-40-50-6(b). The functional area is Manufacturing/COGS and the cost element type is Primary Costs, both supporting classification as direct material purchases. The substantial balance of $7.4M indicates this is a core inventory purchase account for manufacturing operations.

This account represents purchased semi-finished goods which are materials acquired for further processing or assembly into finished products for resale. Under ASC 220-40-50-6(b), purchases of inventory specifically includes raw materials and goods purchased for resale, and semi-finished goods clearly fall into this category as they are inventory items that will be converted to finished goods. The functional area of Manufacturing/COGS and classification as Primary Costs further confirms this represents direct material costs that should be classified as purchases of inventory and presented in COGS.

Packaging materials used in manufacturing are direct materials that become part of the finished product or are necessary for product delivery to customers. These materials are purchased inventory components that should be classified under the purchases of inventory category. The functional area designation as Manufacturing/COGS and the substantial balance of $946,319 confirms this is a primary cost component of goods sold, not a period expense.

Auxiliary Materials - Production represents materials used directly in the manufacturing process, which qualifies as purchases of inventory under ASC 220-40-50-6(b). The account description clearly indicates production-related materials, and the functional area classification as Manufacturing/COGS confirms this is part of the cost of goods sold. The substantial balance of $925,884 is consistent with a material component of production costs that should be classified as inventory purchases.
